disease_burden_data.name: CDC Tick Bite Data Tracker (ER Visits for Tick Bites)
disease_burden_data.description: National syndromic surveillance of emergency department visits for tick bites — by week, month, region, age, sex. Best real-time proxy for disease burden.
disease_burden_data.notes: 2026 spring data: highest ER visit rates since 2017 in Northeast + Midwest. South Central region lower in spring but still elevated overall. No direct county download, but regional trends fill South gap when combined with AGS data.

disease_burden_data.name: CDC MMWR: Geographic Distribution of Suspected Alpha-gal Syndrome (2017–2022)
disease_burden_data.description: 357k+ lab tests showing suspected AGS cases by county. Highest per-capita rates in South/Midwest: Charlotte County VA (12,273 per 1M PPY), Muhlenberg County KY (6,107), plus heavy clustering in AR, MO, OK, TN, MS.
disease_burden_data.notes: Direct evidence South is NOT empty — contiguous hotspot across southern/midwestern/mid-Atlantic states where Lone Star ticks dominate.

disease_burden_data.name: Alpha-gal IgE Prevalence in U.S. Military Recruits (2024 study)
disease_burden_data.description: 6.0% overall positivity; highest in AR (39%), OK (35%), MO (29%). Closely tracks Lone Star tick range.
disease_burden_data.notes: Strong South burden indicator — undercounts clinical cases but shows massive exposure in the South that traditional Lyme surveillance misses.

disease_burden_data.name: Arkansas Alpha-gal Syndrome Mandatory Reporting (2023–2025)
disease_burden_data.description: First state to make AGS reportable: 304 cases (2023), 2,613 (2024), 3,781 (2025). 5,167 reported through July 2025 across 49/75 counties.
disease_burden_data.notes: Concrete numbers filling the South 'empty' zone — northern AR is a major hotspot.

disease_burden_data.name: Missouri Alpha-gal Syndrome Official Estimate
disease_burden_data.description: 7,300–34,400 Missourians affected since 2021 (some seroprevalence extrapolations reach ~80k). New 2026 bill makes it reportable.
disease_burden_data.notes: Direct counter to low reported Lyme in MO — real burden is AGS/Ehrlichia, not Lyme.

disease_burden_data.name: AGS Healthcare Utilization Studies (Anaphylaxis & ER/Hospital Burden)
disease_burden_data.description: Multiple studies show >50% of AGS patients experience anaphylaxis requiring ED visits (some multiple hospitalizations). Delayed diagnosis drives high costs.
disease_burden_data.notes: Underdiagnosed AGS in South/Midwest leads to repeated ER visits misdiagnosed as idiopathic anaphylaxis.

disease_burden_data.name: California Lyme Annual Report 2024
disease_burden_data.description: 96 reported cases statewide (low incidence, mostly coastal/Sierra foothills).
disease_burden_data.notes: West Coast contrast: low overall burden vs. South AGS dominance.

disease_burden_data.name: Oregon & Washington Lyme Incidence (low)
disease_burden_data.description: Oregon ~61 cases (2023); Washington ~25 cases (2023) — mostly travel-related.
disease_burden_data.notes: West Coast stays low on both Lyme and AGS.

generated_for: thisticksmeoff — disease burden (ER visits + AGS/Lyme infections)
notes_for_pipeline: Use CDC Tick Bite Tracker for regional ER heat. Overlay AGS reporting + seroprevalence for South (AR/MO/OK/KY) to fill the 'empty' zone. Lyme county CSV for contrast. This shows true burden: South = AGS-heavy, Midwest = mixed, West Coast = low.
